title: rubus × hemimacrophyllus
intro: Rubus × hemimacrophyllus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Rubus armeniacus and Rubus bifrons. The plant contains anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus × hemimacrophyllus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions.
